en.name: N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) is the biologically inactive 76-amino-acid N-terminal fragment of proBNP, produced by ventricular cardiomyocytes. Plasma NT-proBNP rises in heart failure and is widely used to rule out the diagnosis in patients presenting with dyspnoea [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by immunoassay. Results are reported as a mass concentration in pg/mL (or ng/L — numerically identical) [2]. **Note: NT-proBNP vs BNP.** ProBNP is cleaved into equimolar amounts of the biologically active **BNP** and the biologically inactive **NT-proBNP** [1]. In plasma, NT-proBNP concentrations are roughly 6 times higher than BNP because their clearance mechanisms differ [1]. The two are **not numerically interchangeable** and use different cut-offs. This concept set covers **NT-proBNP only** ; BNP is captured by the separate concept set "B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) in blood". ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on three LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), each expanded to include all their descendants. The set resolves to five LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma - *3029187 / 33762-6 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *42529224 / 83107-3 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay (LOINC)* - *3029435 / 33763-4 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: SI form. - *42529225 / 83108-1 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay (LOINC)*: SI form, immunoassay. ### Whole blood - *42870364 / 71425-3 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Blood by Immunoassay (LOINC)* - *1469712 / 106735-4 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Rapid immunoassay (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`pg/mL`** (concept 8845 — picogram per millilitre) for `[Mass/volume]` codes and **`pmol/L`** (concept 8729 — picomole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes. `1 pg/mL = 1 ng/L` (numerically equal). The stoichiometric conversion factor is `1 pg/mL ≈ 0.118 pmol/L`, derived from the NT-proBNP molecular weight of ~ 8500 Da (the 76-amino-acid N-terminal fragment of proBNP — sequence from UniProt P16860). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits: - **Pleural fluid, peritoneal dialysis fluid specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports an NT-proBNP value: `3029187 / 33762-6 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source reports BNP**: do **not** map. Use "B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) in blood" instead. - **Do not confuse with**: BNP (separate concept set, different molecule and cut-offs), MR-proANP (mid-regional pro-atrial natriuretic peptide). ## References [1] Novack ML, Zubair M. Natriuretic Peptide B Type Test. In: *StatPearls*. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing. Available: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK556136/ [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 33762-6 — Natriuretic peptide.B prohormone N-Terminal [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/33762-6/
